name: label-scope-not-reconcile-counters
description: When multiple CLI views report a related metric with intentionally different scopes (per-agent vs system-wide vs read-marking), label scope inline in human output ('Mail: N unread (to <agent>)') and document scope + side effects in CLI --help. Reconciling counters into one global value is wrong when the views serve different purposes; documenting scope is sufficient.

content: Watchdog health.ts distinguishes 'completed but missed signal' from 'true zombie' via HealthCheck.action='complete'. When tmux/pid is dead AND lastActivity > staleMs AND state is active (working|booting|stalled), evaluateHealth returns action='complete' with state='completed'; the daemon then calls tryTransitionState(completed) without killing or notifying parents. Recent activity + observable death still maps to action='terminate' (true crash). transitionState bypasses the forward-only STATE_ORDER guard for action='complete' since completed (order 2) is before stalled (order 3). (overstory-e74b)
